disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Eruption began within 24 hours of shaving the lower legs, followed by tight occlusive clothing (workout leggings) causing friction over the shaved area
trigger_question_examples: Did anything happen just before the spots appeared — shaving, waxing, new products?
What were you doing around the time this came up?
Had you shaved or worn anything tight on your legs recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I'd shaved my legs that evening and then put on my tight workout leggings straight after. The spots came up overnight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lesions are follicle-centred pustules on a discrete erythematous base with completely normal, uninflamed skin between them — no confluent spreading erythema
trigger_question_examples: Does each spot have a hair in the middle, and is the skin between the spots normal?
Are the spots separate little bumps, or is the redness joining up into one big patch?
Can you describe exactly what each spot looks like up close?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you say it — yes, nearly every spot has a hair poking out of the middle, and the skin in between is totally normal, not red or joined up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient applied a heavy occlusive body butter over the affected area, worsening follicular occlusion
trigger_question_examples: Have you put any creams, oils, or lotions on the area?
What have you been using on your skin since this started?
Did you apply any moisturiser or product over the spots?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I slathered a really thick body butter over it to try to soothe it, but honestly it seemed to make it worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No systemic features — denies fever, chills, rigors, or feeling generally unwell
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills, shivers, or felt unwell in yourself?
Do you feel hot and cold or run down at all?
Any temperature or feeling sick alongside the rash?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no fever or chills, I feel completely fine in myself, just worried about the spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No expanding confluent hot red patch, no red streaks tracking up the limb, and no large deep fluctuant painful lump
trigger_question_examples: Is any of the redness spreading into a large hot patch, or are there red streaks running up your leg?
Have any spots turned into a big, deep, very painful lump?
Is the redness getting bigger or joining together, or streaking upward?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no big red patch spreading and no streaks going up my leg, and none of them have turned into a big deep lump — they're all still little separate spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High health anxiety — patient specifically fears a serious spreading or drug-resistant (MRSA) infection
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
Is there something specific you're afraid of here?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm terrified it's a serious staph or one of those drug-resistant infections you read about — it came up so fast.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need antibiotic tablets or to go to the hospital for this? I'm scared it'll spread while I wait.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure proportionately: explain this looks like a benign, self-limiting follicular irritation/infection that does not need hospital or routine oral antibiotics; give clear self-care and hygiene advice; and give specific safety-net signs that WOULD warrant urgent review, without either dismissing the concern or over-escalating.
